sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.sectionTitle: Latin American and Caribbean Studies – Major
sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.rawLine: The Latin American and Caribbean Studies major combines language study in Spanish and Portuguese with work in history, the humanities, and the social sciences. It provides a comprehensive, interdisciplinary understanding of this major world region, emphasizing themes such as economic development, popular movements, critical analysis, and hemispheric relations.

sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.rawLine: Explores the Americas' evolving political, economic, and social relationships since the 1900s. Focuses on state policies of genocide, extraction, and colonial occupation, as well as social practices of migration and transnational movements of resistance and solidarity. sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.rawLine: Sociological examination of the concepts of undocumented, citizen, and the structuring of (il)legality as they are situated in axes of power, specifically in racialized and gendered contexts. Institutional outcomes in migration, law, labor, education, carceral spaces, and health.

sourceSectionFilterAuditRows.rawLine: History and Development of Afro-Peruvian Dance analyzes the historical development of Afro-Peruvian dance practices. Explores dance within the colonial and post-colonial contexts in which these traditions emerged and continue to evolve in the Andean Region and as part of a larger Afro-Latin America. Course materials and discussions involve issues of race, class, power, nationalism, cultural resistance and revival, folklorization, migration and globalization.

displayCourseCodes: HSTCMP 247 Global Health Histories: Colonial Medicine, Public Health, And International Health In The Global South DIV, SSc 5
options.title: Traces the roots of the modern global health movement by examining the history of overseas interventions in medicine and public health from the fifteenth century to the present. Focuses primarily on Latin America while including case studies on Africa, Asia, Australia, and the Pacific.
options.sourceHeading: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces
options.sourceCategory: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces
options.label: HSTCMP 247 Global Health Histories: Colonial Medicine, Public Health, And International Health In The Global South DIV, SSc 5

displayCourseCodes: HSTLAC 185 Race, Gender, And Class In Latin America And The Caribbean DIV, SSc 5
options.title: Explores the power struggles generated by imperial expansion, colonialism, and processes of nation-formation (1400 to the present) in the forging of Latin America and the Caribbean. Examines how gender, race, sex, and class became crucial means to sustain, navigate, and challenge domination as well as to imagine alternative futures.
options.sourceHeading: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces
options.sourceCategory: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces
options.label: HSTLAC 185 Race, Gender, And Class In Latin America And The Caribbean DIV, SSc 5

displayCourseCodes: HSTLAC 280 Drug Wars In Latin America DIV, SSc 5
options.title: Analyses "War on Drugs" in Latin America as political, economic, and socio-cultural construct. Investigates local, regional and global dynamics rendering some mood-altering substances as legal while subjecting others to prohibitionist policies at different historical times. Explores racial, class and gender logics shaping these processes. Examines how informal economies are well entrenched within longstanding, transnational institutions.
options.sourceHeading: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces
options.sourceCategory: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces
options.label: HSTLAC 280 Drug Wars In Latin America DIV, SSc 5

displayCourseCodes: HSTLAC 282 History Of Mexico: Culture, Identity, And The Politics Of Rule From The Aztecs To The Present DIV, SSc 5
options.title: Overview of Mexican history from late Aztec times until the twenty-first century. Emphasizes how women, campesinos, indigenous populations, free and enslaved Afro-Mexicans, and the urban poor experienced the past, challenged colonial and post-colonial rule, and shaped modern Mexican society and culture.
options.sourceHeading: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces

options.label: HSTLAC 282 History Of Mexico: Culture, Identity, And The Politics Of Rule From The Aztecs To The Present DIV, SSc 5

displayCourseCodes: HSTLAC 481 History Of Peru And The Andean Region SSc 5
options.title: Traces the history of Peru specifically and the Andean highlands generally, from Inca times to the present, examining the shifting relationship between peasants, indigenous peoples, and the state. Analyzes historically why modern forms of peasant and indigenous political power differs radically between Peru, Bolivia, and Ecuador.

displayCourseCodes: GEOG 403 Transnational Latinx Migrations DIV, SSc 5
options.title: Explores the role of gender, racial formation, and language in transnational Latin American migrations. Outlines key concepts related to power relations in nation-states, geographies of security, border enforcement, and the production of Latinidades, or multiple Latinx identities.
